# Mission, Vision, Values
[About](https://www.sunmarke.com/about/ "Go to About.") > Mission, Vision, Values
# Mission, Vision, Values 
Inspiring Students to Thrive and Grow Every Day 
## Our Vision
_‘Empowering young people through an innovative and character-building education that inspires them to thrive and achieve with purpose.’_
## Our Mission
_‘Nurturing the next generation of changemakers for a better tomorrow.’_
## Our Values
We ASPIRE to uphold the following values:
![Accountability](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0NzoxMjcx-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNTAgNTAiIHdpZHRoPSI1MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
### Accountability
We believe in taking responsibility and leading by example
![](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0ODo5ODc=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNTAgNTAiIHdpZHRoPSI1MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
### Service
We believe in helping colleagues, giving back to our community and serving society
![](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0OTo5ODY=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNTAgNTAiIHdpZHRoPSI1MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
### Purpose
We believe in living a life of greater purpose and in making a positive impact on society
![Integrity](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MDoxMjcx-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNTAgNTAiIHdpZHRoPSI1MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
### Integrity
We believe in upholding the highest standards of ethical conduct in thought, word and deed
![Respect](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MTo5NTc=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNTAgNTAiIHdpZHRoPSI1MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
### Respect
We recognise our differences as well as our commonalities and believe in treating one another with courtesy and respect
![](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1Mjo5NTI=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNTAgNTAiIHdpZHRoPSI1MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
### Excellence
We strive to be the best we can be and in giving our 100%
## British Values
As a British Schools Overseas (BSO) accredited school, Sunmarke School is committed to upholding the values and standards of a world-class British education. This includes a strong focus on the spiritual, moral, social and cultural development of our students, as well as the promotion of respect, tolerance, and responsibility. These principles align seamlessly with the values of the UAE, where respect for diversity and cultural understanding form the foundation of daily life. Together, these shared values shape our school community into one that is inclusive, aspirational, and globally minded.
  * **Rule of Law** – School policies, codes of conduct, and classroom expectations help pupils understand fairness, responsibility, and the importance of living within agreed rules, reflecting the laws of the UAE.
  * **Individual Liberty** – We encourage independence and student choice in learning, while ensuring safety and guidance so pupils understand the balance between rights and responsibilities.
  * **Mutual Respect** – Our Positive Education ethos, house system, and community partnerships promote kindness and respect in daily interactions across the school.
  * **Tolerance of Different Faiths and Beliefs** – We celebrate the UAE’s cultural and religious diversity, providing opportunities to learn about and respect different traditions while remaining rooted in our own values.
  * **Student Voice & Representation** – Pupils are given a voice through Student Council, Sports Leaders, and other leadership opportunities, ensuring they understand the importance of fairness, collaboration, and shared decision-making.


## Why Educate Your Child at Sunmarke?
**A Rigorous & Ambitious Education**  
We offer rigorous, ambitious and holistic education emphasising academic excellence and participation in diverse extracurricular activities enabling children to achieve outstanding academic, social and personal success in a stimulating and supportive environment.
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg4ODoxMDIz-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
**Outstanding Senior Leaders & Teachers**  
All our inspiring senior leaders, teachers and staff are trained in Positive Education and best practices in education, and are provided continuous professional development and training to keep abreast of the latest developments in pedagogy.
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg5MDo4Nzg=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
**Over Four Decades of History**  
Built on the foundation of Fortes Education’s four decades of excellence in education, Sunmarke’s dynamic management team bring their rich experience and knowledge to deliver the best value-based education for students.

# Our Approach
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [EYFS](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/eyfs/ "Go to EYFS.") > Our Approach
# EYFS Curriculum
# Our Approach
Laying The Foundations For A Lifelong Love Of Learning 
A child’s journey of education begins in the Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) department at Sunmarke. Blending a range of outstanding Early Years practices from the best international schools, we commit to a child-initiated learning approach and a sophisticated play-based curriculum that enables students to fulfil their unique potential.
![EYFS_Our-Approach-2](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0MzoxNjM=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjE1IDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYxNSIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
![EYFS_Our-Approach-STEAM](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MjoxNjc=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjE1IDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYxNSIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
#### Sunmarke’s Amazing Philosophy and Journey 
**A-** Autonomy: 
Children are central to everything we do; all projects stem directly from their interests.
**M** – Mindset: 
In line with Sunmarke’s ‘Positive Education’ ethos, we teach children to have a ‘Growth Mindset’, encouraging endless possibilities. 
**A-** Academics: 
We provide a broad, balanced yet innovative British curriculum based on the Early Years Foundation Stage, building the bedrock for the future.
**Z** -Zest: 
Happiness is a key agenda at Sunmarke School; we truly believe that if a child is happy and enthusiastic, they learn better.
**I** – Individuality: 
Our personalised curriculum ensures every child’s individuality is recognised. Strong teacher/pupil relationships allows tutors to identify and cater to a child’s interests, strengths and challenges.
**N** – Nurture: 
We nurture every child to enable them to grow and reach their fullest potential.
**G** – Grow: 
Every child that enters EYFS has a special and incredible journey that helps them grow and develop into happy, healthy teens.
#### Learning Through Play
Imaginative and creative play is how children learn about the world. During imaginative play, they manipulate materials, express themselves verbally and non-verbally, plan, act, interact, react and try different roles.
Learning is most effective when children participate in creative play with dolls, vehicles, blocks, rocks, cardboard, or boxes. Employing creative thinking while manipulating play dough, creating recipes by mixing dirt and water, working with art materials, splashing in puddles, or pretending to fly can further child development.
Imagination fosters cognitive and social development by allowing children to contemplate different resolutions, thus boosting their self-confidence.
Our Early Years Foundation Stage has been rated as ‘**Outstanding** ’ in the latest KHDA’s DSIB inspection report (2023-24) with ‘**Outstanding** Personal Development & Learning Skills and **Outstanding** Curriculum and Curriculum Adaptation’.

# Re-enrol Online
[Admissions](https://www.sunmarke.com/admissions/ "Go to Admissions.") > Re-enrol Online
# Re-enrol Online
Procedure To Continue Your Educational Journey
Sunmarke owes its success as an international school offering the best British curriculum education in Dubai to the constant support and confidence families have reposed in us.
We encourage students to aim for the stars but we equally hold ourselves to high standards, committed to offering the best academic provision and value-for-money education in the UAE and larger MENA region.
To continue your journey with us and to secure a seat for the academic year 2025-26, we encourage all our families to re-enrol online. A non-refundable re-enrolment fee of 5% must be paid by April 30, 2026, and will be deducted from the tuition fee due for the term of joining. Please note that if the re-enrolment fee is not paid by April 30, 2026, the entire Term 1 fee will be invoiced (subject to availability).
To re-enrol your child, please click the below link and log on to the [VLE portal](https://vle.forteseducation.com) on your desktop or VLE Mobile App, and complete the Re-enrolment process online.
[Re-enrol](https://vle.forteseducation.com/)

# Primary Curriculum
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Primary](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/primary/ "Go to Primary.") > Primary Curriculum
# Primary School
# Primary Curriculum
A Curriculum Underpinned by our Positive Education Ethos
## Philosophy
At Sunmarke, we firmly believe in equipping our students with the skills they’ll need in the ever-changing landscape of 21st Century thinking and work. We do this by empowering our students through an innovative and character-building education that inspires them to thrive and achieve with purpose. Our curriculum design serves to reflect this. Whilst maintaining academic rigour through the British National Curriculum, our holistic Positive Education ethos and ambitious commitment to innovative technology ensures our children achieve outstanding academic, social and personal success. Additionally, opportunities for Forest School lessons and outdoor learning are provided to all Primary students.
Therefore, the drivers for our curriculum are based on two principles: all learning is **purposeful** with real-life application and context; and students can **make connections** across interdisciplinary skills.
### STEAM
With the opening of our dedicated STEAM Centre, SPACE-X, and the addition of specialist resident design technology and computing teachers, we have redesigned our curriculum to take us from our thematic roots to design thinking, using the Stanford d.school approach below. 
[![Our-Curriculum---page-image](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0MToyODQ=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgODAwIDMwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjgwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIzMDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)](https://sunmarke.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Our-Curriculum-page-image.jpg)
Whilst English and maths remain explicitly on the timetable, children work on STEAM theme projects across science, design technology, engineering, the arts, maths and humanities, with the option to also make purposeful links to our UAE moral, social and cultural studies and Values in Action objectives. Living in the UAE, it is vital we also make purposeful links to our local surroundings, Islamic culture and Arabic language. This hands-on approach to problem-solving enables each STEAM theme to culminate in a proposed project which requires the students to design/make/create something which draws upon the knowledge and skills which they have learnt over the course of the project. During each STEAM project, each class will spend two weeks in SPACE-X, where the support of our specialists will enable children’s designs to come to life.   
| Primary Core Subjects  | Primary Foundation Subjects  |  
| --- | --- |  
| English  | History  |  
| Mathematics  | Geography  |  
| STEAM (including science)  | Modern Foreign languages (MFL): French/Mandarin  |  
| Arabic  | Art & Design  |  
| Islamic  | Computing  |  
| Values in Action/moral, social and cultural studies  | Music  |  
|   | Physical Education (including swimming)  |  
In addition to the subjects above, Sunmarke offers a diverse range of sports, co-curricular and enrichment activities to supplement the academic programme.

# Scholarships

# Sunmarke Fellows
Our Scholarship Programme for Gifted & Talented Students
The Fortes Scholarship Programme is a recognition of ability and application of students who have not only demonstrated a high level of academic, sporting or artistic skills, but have also displayed motivation and drive to succeed.
## Inspiring Purpose. Driving Passion.
With exceptional teachers and subject specialists, we provide a dynamic and innovative curriculum that is designed to meet and inspire the talents and interests of our Gifted & Talented scholars.  

Our approach helps students stretch their abilities, build confidence, and pursue advanced pathways that match their strengths. We encourage curiosity, creativity, and independence so each scholar can excel in areas where they show remarkable potential.

# FAQs
#### What are the available Scholarship Categories?
Currently, our Fortes Schools are offering the following scholarship categories:
  * **Academic Excellence Scholarship**  
Awarded to high-achieving students with exceptional academic records, strong critical thinking skills, and ambitions to join top global universities.


  * **Sports Scholarship**  
For students who demonstrate outstanding skill, dedication, and achievement in competitive sports at school, club, or national level.


  * **Performing & Creative Arts Scholarship**  
This scholarship supports talented students in the performing and/or visual arts — including drama, music, dance, and visual art — who show creative excellence and commitment to their craft.


  * **Innovation & Future Readiness Scholarship**  
For students with demonstrated interest and potential in emerging fields such as AI, sustainability, entrepreneurship, or innovation – those ready to shape the future.


  * **Founder’s Scholarship**  
This scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional potential in any one area – or across a combination of academics, leadership, innovation, community impact, or personal achievement. It’s designed for individuals whose unique strengths and contributions set them apart.


![Scholarships](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg3MjoxNTY=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjAwIDMwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIzMDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)

#### What are the Scholarship Criteria?
Do keep the following documents ready before you fill in the application form:
**Criteria for All Scholarships**
  * 2 references
  * 3 Essay questions


  *     * Personal statement (< 500 words)


  *     * Scholarship question (<500 words)


  *     * Leadership essay (< 500 words)


  * Video presentation (< 7 mins)  
Video must have:


  *     * Introduction to yourself


  *     * Why do you deserve this scholarship?


**Additional Criteria for Academic Excellence Scholarship**
  * CAT score = or > 133
  * For students applying for Year 10, 11 and 13: GL Data in Y9 and Y10 to be Stanine 8 or above in English, Maths and or Science
  * For students applying for Year 12: 10 or more GCSEs taken with scores of 7-9’s or A to A*


**Additional Criteria for Sports Scholarships**
  * The student should have achieved national/international sporting representative honours in at least 1 of the following sports – Football, Swimming, Basketball, Cricket, Netball, Golf, Rugby, Volleyball, Athletics, Tennis. Valid for the last 2 years or any notable awards received.


**Additional Criteria for Performing & Creative Arts Scholarship**
  * Grade 6 (or above) in at least one instrument or voice, through Trinity or ABRSM, achieved within the last 3 years (3 Minute Performance Video)
  * Video evidence of a solo live performance outside school
  * A portfolio with evidence of exhibition (internal and/or external)


**Additional Criteria for Innovation & Future Readiness Scholarship**
  * Portfolio and evidence of having a future skill. The skill can be related to Coding, Crypto, Social Work, Robotics, etc.
  * Certifications/endorsements required.


**Additional Criteria for Founder’s Scholarship**
  * Recognition in areas such as sports, arts, STEAM, or other exceptional accomplishments.
  * Proven leadership skills through school or community involvement, extracurricular activities, or innovative projects.
  * Consistently high performance across subjects, demonstrating strong intellectual ability and a passion for learning.


#### What do we expect from a scholar?
We would like to set expectations with the scholar on performance during the scholarship year. This is essential for the Scholarship Performance Review.
**For All Scholars**
  * Consistent commitment to growth and development
  * Active participation in internal and external activities. For example, the World Scholar’s Cup, MUN, TEDx, Duke of Edinburgh, Internships, social work among others
  * To act as a brand ambassador of the school across all platforms. For example, social media, PR, videos, etc.


**For Academic Excellence Scholarship**
  * To be in the 95 percentile of their Year Group (for Year 10 and 12)
  * To achieve Level 7-9 in all their GCSE subjects (for Year 11)


**For Sports Scholars**
  * Maintain national/international representation in the sport or participate and represent the school in a notable tournament
  * To be in the 70 percentile of their Year Group (for Year 10 and 12)
  * To achieve Level 6-9 in all their GCSE subjects (for Year 11)


**For Performing & Creative Arts Scholarship**
  * Participate in **school showcases** (concerts, musicals, productions)
  * Contribute to **bands, choirs, ensembles**
  * Be in the **top 75 percent** of their year group (Years 10 and 12)
  * For GCSE Art, Music and Drama related subjects: achieve _A or grades 8 to 9_ *
  * Maintain a **strong portfolio** and evidence of external exhibition (where relevant)


**For Innovation & Future Readiness Scholarship**
  * Maintain an exceptional portfolio of future skill
  * To be in the 80 percentile of their Year Group (for Year 10 and 12)
  * To achieve Level 7-9 in all their GCSE subjects (for Year 11)


**For Founder’s Scholarship**
  * Show initiative, inspire peers, and take responsibility in school and community activities.
  * Consistently demonstrate strong academic performance and a love for learning.
  * Contribute positively to the school and wider community through service and participation.
  * Work effectively with others, valuing diverse perspectives and ideas.


#### What will we offer?
Our Fortes School will create an environment conducive for a scholar’s growth. A Fortes School will:
**For All Scholars**
  * Ensure that the student receives guidance to develop a strong character and become a well-rounded individual
  * Ensure the student has adequate support to perform academically
  * Ensure opportunities to explore leadership positions within the Student Leadership Team
  * Ensure opportunities to participate in activities outside the curriculum


**For Academic Excellence Scholars**
  * Provide an environment that encourages the student to achieve the highest academic standards


**For Sports Scholars**
  * Provide an environment that ensures the student is able to maintain and improve performance
  * Ensure that the student has access to facilities to practise and improve skills


**For Performing & Creative Arts Scholars**
  * Provide an environment that ensures the student is able to maintain and improve performance
  * Ensure that the student has access to facilities to practise and improve skills
  * Provide an environment that ensures the student is able to maintain and improve performance
  * Ensure that the student has access to facilities to practise and improve skills


**For Innovation & Future Readiness Scholars**
  * Provide an environment that encourages the student to achieve the highest academic standards
  * Ensure that the student receives opportunities to meet mentors in the field of interest


#### Students from which Year Groups are eligible for a Scholarship?
Students in Year 9 (British Curriculum) or equivalent in other curriculums applying for Year 10 and upwards are eligible for scholarships.
#### Where to apply for the Fortes Scholarship Programme?
_New Student to the School_
  * **After filling out the Admission Application Form**  
Complete the admission application form to Fortes School. At the end of the application form, select the button to ‘Apply for Scholarship’. You will be directed to the Scholarship Form.
  * **Request for the Scholarship Form**  
You can also request for the Scholarship Form from the admissions team, who will send you a unique link. However, the Application Form is mandatory in this case as well.


**_Sunmarke Student_**
  * You may apply for the scholarship via the VLE where you will find a link to the Scholarship Application Form. [Click here](https://vle.forteseducation.com/) to log in.


#### What to expect after submitting the Scholarship Form?
  1. **Application Review** : Your application will be reviewed by our Academic Panel
  2. **Audition** : Should you get shortlisted, you will be invited to the school for an audition
  3. **Interview** : If you make it as a finalist, you will be invited for an interview with the Principal of the school


Finally, your application will be assessed and successful applicants could receive a partial scholarship on the annual fees.
#### Is the Scholarship only for the Year of admission or all subsequent years?
A scholarship is renewable yearly between Year 10 and Year 13. The student’s scholarship continuation is subject to a termly Panel Review that showcases his/her participation, growth and development**.**

# The Achievement Centre – Inclusion Department
[About](https://www.sunmarke.com/about/ "Go to About.") > The Achievement Centre – Inclusion Department
# The Achievement Centre – Inclusion Department
Making Learning Accessible To All 
Inclusivity is enshrined in Sunmarke’s ethos where we welcome a diverse range of learners from EYFS to Secondary. The Achievement Centre takes the lead in providing an inclusive education to students in close collaboration with parents, teachers and external specialists.
“
Jayne West 
Head Of Inclusion
Laila is a Global Ambassador representing sports for People of Determination worldwide. She was an outstanding member of the Secondary Life Skills Class, where she gained the qualifications to be accepted into Dubai College of Leisure and Tourism further education course. We are all very proud of her as she continues to inspire our students to believe in the dream!!!
To facilitate the successful participation and progress of all learners, a range of additional provisions, subject to staffing and availability, are offered at no extra cost. They include, but are not limited to:
  * Interventions for literacy
  * Interventions for mathematics
  * Speaking and listening
  * Social skills
  * Motor skills
  * Directed studies
  * Extra-curricular activities specific to students with SEND (Special Educational Needs & Disabilities)


## Commitment to Inclusion
  * Our school is also an accredited centre for the BTEC Introductory Awards offering a wide range of both academic and vocational units including Business Administration, Media, Health and Fitness, Leisure and Hospitality.
  * It has a highly successful SEND department which has been commended in our most recent DSIB (Dubai School Inspection Bureau) inspection.
  * We have developed a reliable network of external providers, who parents can contract to provide specialist assessment and therapy services at Sunmarke. These include, but are not limited to:


  1. Comprehensive developmental and diagnostic assessment
  2. Psycho-educational assessment
  3. Behavioural assessment and intervention
  4. Occupational Therapy and Sensory Integration assessment and intervention
  5. Speech and Language Therapy assessment and intervention
  6. Personalised support from a 1:1 Learning Support Assistant


## Fortes English Language Programme
The Fortes English Language Programme is an intensive programme of cross curricular, full immersion pedagogy, with a focus on academic English, alongside every day language and culture.
Students learn language alongside new skills and all within a broad range of subjects and activities such as Art, Drama, PE and Sport, Music in addition to the usual core subjects. Students experience accelerated language acquisition and achieve a deeper understanding of subject-specific English across the curriculum.

# FAQs
[Admissions](https://www.sunmarke.com/admissions/ "Go to Admissions.") > FAQs
# Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ’s) 
All You Want To Know About The School And Its Facilities
We have compiled a list of questions that parents are most likely to ask us in the early stages of the application process – we hope you find it useful. Should you have any other questions, please get in touch with our Family Happiness Team and we will be happy to help.
####  [ ](https://www.sunmarke.com/admissions/faqs/)
#### Enrolment for 2024-25 – New families
**1) Can I come in for a school tour with my family?**
Yes, we would be delighted to welcome you and your family for a school tour. It’s a great opportunity to experience the atmosphere and gain insight into our school.  
**  
2) KHDA Parent School Contract**
Please note that:
Students can only attend school if they are registered in the KHDA School System, and parents have signed the Parent-School Contract.
The school will register the student once all the relevant documents have been uploaded by the parents.
This contract needs to be digitally signed only once – by parents of students who are new to the school. The contract is usually sent via sms on their local mobile phones registered with the school.
**3) Documents for Registering at Sunmarke School**
If you are a new family, joining the school, please ensure that all pending documentation for your child is submitted to the Admissions department at Sunmarke School.
**Documents required to complete the registration process:**
  * Latest School Reports
  * School reference letter, if a report is not available
  * Attested Transfer Certificate
  * 1 Passport Size Coloured Photo (Student and both Parents)
  * Student Passport Copy
  * Sponsoring Parents Passport Copy
  * Student’s Emirates ID Card (back & Front)
  * Sponsoring Parent Emirates ID card (back & Front)
  * Vaccination & Immunisation Record Copy


**4) Age Cut-Off Dates**
New students must be enrolled in their respective age groups as per the ministerial decree (820) of 2014.
The cut-off date is 31st August without exception.
The table below shows the age/year-group information of students aged 3 to 6. Sunmarke School is required to comply with the age/year-group criteria and the cut-off date as per the ministerial decree.  
| Year  | FS1  | FS2  | Year 1  | Year 2  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| Age by 31st August  | 3  | 4  | 5  | 6  |  
**This is a federal law and we are not able to consider any exemption requests.**
#### What are the KHDA guidelines for Admissions and Transfers?
Click [here](https://www.sunmarke.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/Admission-and-Transfer-Guidelines.pdf) to read Guidelines for Admissions and Transfers by KHDA.
#### How do I book Assessments?
Admissions are open year-round, with prorated fees available. We are booking assessments for FS1, FS2, Year 1 to 10, and Year 12. For students currently overseas, we can offer online assessments.
Please contact the Sunmarke Admissions Office to confirm your child’s assessment date.
#### Can my child be assessed before arriving in Dubai?
**Online Assessment Procedure:**
The assessment will take place at home under exam conditions via MS Teams. For Year 6 and below, we recommend adult supervision throughout. Ensure your child has a quiet, distraction-free workspace and the necessary equipment: a computer with internet, video, and audio capabilities. All other electronic devices should be switched off.
You will receive an email with the meeting link. Please check access ahead of time and join at the scheduled time. Assistance is only allowed for setting up the meeting and login. Any interference during the assessment will invalidate the results.
Assessment Checklist:
  * Quiet, well-ventilated workspace.
  * Computer with internet, video, and audio.
  * All distractions (TV, phones, etc.) turned off.
  * Microsoft Teams ready to join the meeting.

#### Tuition Fees
Click here to view the [Tuition Fees: New Students - FS1 to Year 13 - 2025-26](https://www.sunmarke.com/admissions/tuition-fees/)
#### What are the KHDA document fees?
Tariff for the following KHDA services as stipulated by KHDA:
  * Request for student Education Continuation Certificate for visa renewal (AED 120)
  * Request for student leaving form for the students who are leaving Dubai (AED 120)
  * Request for student transfer between private schools (within the stipulated time frame)-Student transfer (AED 120)


####  [ ](https://www.sunmarke.com/admissions/faqs/)
At Sunmarke, we exercise a positive and inclusive admissions policy. Candidates will each be given due consideration on a case-by-case basis, and we will take into account the capacity of the premises, staff and curriculum pathways to effectively meet the needs of the individual.
Our Achievement Centre offers a broad range of additional support for students with SEND, including targeted support in maths, literacy, and SEMH (social and emotional mental health). We currently offer an alternative curriculum pathway for Key Stages 3 and 4.
**1. Is it approved by the Ministry of Education?**  
It is a mainstream curriculum, delivered by specialist teachers and LSAs. There is no need for permission for this pathway as it falls under our current education licence.
**2. What are the timings if my son is enroled in this program?**  
The timings are that of the normal school day, unless the student has additional needs or commitments outside of school that need to be met. As a KHDA accredited Rahhal provider, we can review timings to meet the student’s needs.
**3. What is the cost for LSA?**  
Costs are an estimate range until all students are enroled.
**4. What would be mentioned in the invoice for this program? Is it “Tuition fee”?**  
School fees are billed as normal. Parents will then have to sign a KHDA Individual Service Agreement for the LSP cost. This will then be a separate invoice.
**5. How will I enrol my child?**  
Our SENCO will assess the student before we can accept to ensure we can meet their needs in this class. It is a pathway that supports access to the mainstream and we can’t cater for severe needs.

#### What are the age requirements for AY 2024-25?
**1. What are the changes to the age requirements for entry to Sunmarke School?**
Age requirements will change for new students enroled into FS1, according to the UAE Ministry of Education Resolution no (24).
Children enroling into FS1, starting in September, must be three years old on or before the 31st of August.
Children enroling into FS2, starting in September, must be four years old on or before the 31st of August.
Similarly, children enroling into Year 1, starting school in September, must be five years old on or before the 31st of August.
The MOE resolution stipulates that all students currently enroled at private schools in the UAE are not affected by the change. This includes students who enrol for the final term of the 2023/24 academic year.  
Schools that start the academic year in September, in line with MOE Resolution no (24).  
| **Year Group**  | **Age requirement**  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Foundation Stage 1  | 3 years old by August 31st  |  
| Foundation Stage 2  | 4 years old by August 31st  |  
| Year 1  | 5 years old by August 31  |  
| Year 2  | 6 years old by August 31  |  
**2. When did this change come into effect?**
This change was applicable from the start of the 2021/22 academic year in September 2021.
**3. Why was this change made?**
This decision was made to bring more consistency to the cut-off dates used by schools in the UAE with those used by schools abroad. For example, UK schools in the UAE will now have the same cut-off date as schools in the UK, and international British schools.
**4.Who will this change impact?**
This change will impact children up to six years old who are enrolling in school for the first time.
**5. Will the new cut-off date apply to all year groups?**
It will apply to new students enrolling in FS1, FS2, Year 1 and Year 2 in all schools in the UAE, as stipulated in the MOE resolution.
**6. My child was born in October and is now in FS1 – I want to enrol him in another school for FS2. Can I do this or does he have to repeat a year?**
Because your child is already enroled in school, the new cut-off dates in the MOE resolution do not apply. He does not have to repeat the year.
**7. My son was enroled in FS1 this year but I withdrew his enrolment due to COVID-19. He’ll be 3 years old in September. Can I still enrol him in FS2 next year?**
Your child must have formally finished the previous academic year in order to enrol in the next one. Since he does not meet the age requirement for FS2 for next academic year, he must enrol in FS1. According to the MOE resolution, he can enrol in FS2 next academic year only if he completes FS1 this academic year.
**8. My daughter will be 4 years old in December. If I enrol her in FS2 for the last term of this year – before the changes come into effect – will she be able to start Year 1 next year?**
Yes. The new age requirements will apply for new students from the start of the next academic year only. The MOE resolution stipulates that all students currently enroled at private schools in the UAE are not affected by the change.
**9. I’ve already registered and paid the deposit for my daughter to start school in FS2 in September. She will be four years old in November. Is she affected by this?**
According to the new cut-off dates in the MOE resolution, your daughter will not be able to start in FS2 in September. Alternatively, we would be happy to transfer your child’s application to their age-appropriate year group (FS1) and register your child for this year group instead.
Parents whose children are affected by the new cut-off dates and who have already paid deposits for their children’s enrolment are entitled to a refund if they wish.  
**  
10. My child was born one day after the cut-off date. Is there some leeway given?**
No exceptions to the cut-off dates will be given under any circumstances

Bus start times remain the same. For drop-off, buses will leave from the school at the following times  
| Year Group  | Timings  |  
| --- | --- |  
| EYFS  | Monday to Thursday 1:30 pm  
Friday 12:15 pm onwards  |  
| Primary (Year 1 to 6)  | Monday to Thursday 3:30 pm onwards  
Friday 12.15 pm onwards  |  
| Secondary (Year 7 to 13)  | Monday to Thursday 3:30 pm onwards  
Friday 12:15 pm onwards  |  
Further communication on this will be shared by our transportation department.
#### How do I order food for my child? 
**1. How do I book the service?**
Booking can only be done for EYFS and Primary students. Secondary students can go directly to the canteen to select their food and make payment.
EYFS and Primary parents need to pre-book their order in advance via the link provided <http://form.jotform.com/Mastercook/Sunmarke-stripe> and lunch will be delivered in their class.
**2. What is the price for lunch?**
Lunch is priced at AED24.25 this includes the main lunch and a dessert.
**3. What is the menu like?**
Please click [**TERM MENU**](https://www.sunmarke.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/SMS-Revised-School-Menu-Jan-24-2023.pdf) to see current available menu.
Alternatively, secondary students can check out our **GRAB AND GO** menu.
**4. Do you offer special meal plans?**
As a nut-free school, all meals are prepared using nut-free products. We also offer bespoke menus for students with special dietary needs, including gluten-free, dairy-free, etc. For this request, please contact coordinator@mastercook.ae for a specialised price and to discuss options.
**5. Does your menu contain allergen information?**
As per Dubai Municipality guidelines, we follow their standard of menu labels that include the ingredients, allergens, and calorie content. The menu also has a specialised coding that identifies the contents of the meal, allowing our students to check if it contains something they are allergic to. Please communicate this to your child if they are in Secondary school.

**6. Can my child buy food at the Grab & Go counter?**
This option caters only to our Secondary school students and staff. The Grab & Go section of the dining services is exactly what it is – you grab the food, make the payment, and go on with your daily activity. We always ensure that there is a menu suitable for vegetarians and non-vegetarians.
**7. What are the canteen timings?**
The canteen is open from 7am to 3.30 pm Monday to Thursday and 7am to 12:00 noon on Friday.  _Please note that only the**Grab and Go section** of the canteen is open on Friday. _
**8. Is breakfast available in the canteen?**
A healthy breakfast, via the Grab and Go section, is available daily from 7am. Secondary school students can only use the canteen at break time and lunchtime.
**9. What time are the dining services available and when is lunch delivered to EYFS and Primary students?**
The lunch period for various year groups is listed as per the below timetable. For EYFS and Primary students, lunch will be delivered to their classroom prior to their scheduled lunch break. All the delivered lunches will have the names clearly labelled on them so that teachers can easily identify and distribute them to the children during lunchtime.  
| Phases  |   | Mondays to Thursdays  | Fridays  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| Secondary  | Break  | **09:40am – 09:55am**  | **9:40am – 9:55am**  |  
| Lunch  | **12:40pm – 01:40pm**  | Not available  |  
| Primary Key Stage 1 (Y1-2)  | Break  | **09:40am – 09:55pm**  | **9:40am – 9:55am**  |  
| Lunch  | **10:50am – 11:45am**  | Not available  |  
| Primary Key Stage 2 (Y3- 6)  | Break  |  **09:40am – 09:55am**  | **9:40am – 9:55am**  |  
| Lunch  | **11:45am – 12:40pm**  | Not available  |  
| EYFS  | Break  |  **09:40am – 09:55am**  | Not available  |  
| Lunch  | **From 11:45am**  | Not available  |  
**10. How do I pay for my child’s lunch?**
For EYFS and Primary students, parents can pay upon confirming their order. For secondary, payments can be made using their credit card/apple pay / google pay/cash.
**11. What if I forgot to place order can you still give my child her/his food?**
We understand that parents can get busy and at times forget to make lunch, for this one time emergency situation, we suggest you contact Mastercook – Person in Charge- Ms Karen directly on [+971566180756](tel:+971566180756) and she will be happy to assist you for the day.  
**  
12. Who do I contact for any queries, suggestions, and clarifications?**
The Mastercook catering team is happy to assist parents with their queries and for clarification. Please reach out to Karen from Mastercook on [+971 52 618 0756](tel:+971526180756) (mobile number) [+971 4 448 8103](tel:+97144488103) (Mastercook Office #) [+971 4 423 8995](tel:+97144238995) (school number) or email enquiries to coordinator@mastercook.ae
**13. I am travelling for 3 days and would like to book my child lunch during the time I am away, can I place order for 3 days only?**
We are pleased to inform you that Mastercook are now accepting orders for short period, please go to the booking form and indicate the days you will require your child’s lunch delivered.
**14. My child forgot to bring money, can you give him food?**
Mastercook believe that food is essential part of child’s growth development. If your child doesn’t have money for the day, we will issue them the food for you to pay later. Our canteen supervisor will contact parents to confirm this and check for any allergies.

# Enriched Learning
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Primary](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/primary/ "Go to Primary.") > Enriched Learning
# Enriched Learning
Extra-Curricular Activities to Expand Horizons beyond the Timetabled Curriculum
These school-run activities conducted by our academic staff vary from term to term and year to year depending on children’s choice and staffing. Clubs are held from Monday to Thursday after school. A typical range of clubs and activities includes:
#### Year 2
**Fine Motor Skills**
Fine motor club is an opportunity to develop your fine motor skills and communication skills by working with partners. Students will take part in a range of creative activities which support in fine motor development and handwriting skills.
**Mathletics Club**
An amazing opportunity for students to develop a lifelong love of maths through:
  * Confidence with maths facts fluency
  * Enjoyment of solving maths problems
  * Tackling maths puzzles/games
  * Fun with maths song and play


**Yoga & Mindfulness**
At Sunmarke, well-being and positivity are at the heart of everything we do. In this yoga and mindfulness club, students get the chance to end the day calmly with some relaxing activities that promote mindfulness, well-being and appreciation.
**Multi Sports**
Want to get active and have some fun? This club allows children to enjoy energetic activities while being active and blowing off some steam. Students will focus on developing basic movement skills through fun games while coming together to make new friends and work in teams.
**Fun with French**
This club promotes the love of language and the amazing experiences that come along with learning. Students will get the chance to become more confident in French and learn new vocabulary through games and engaging activities.
**Board Games**
Strategy and focus are major skills of almost every board game. In this activity, students have the chance to develop these skills in an interactive group setting. Have a favourite board game or one that you think people wouldn’t have played before? Bring it along to Board Games Club and enjoy developing your critical thinking, problem-solving and strategic skills.
**Photography Club**
If you would like to develop your photography and creative outlook, this is an excellent activity for you. During this club, you will learn about what good photography requires and how to edit and improve your images. Come explore the artist within!
**Art Club**
Art club gives students the chance to learn and develop art skills, using drawing and sketching techniques you will learn to create inventive and creative artwork. Work alongside other talented students to enhance your creative flair and enjoy learning new things.
#### Year 3 & Year 4
**Book Worms**
In Book Worms, students will be able to develop an inner love of reading. In this club, you will read both individually and as a group, writing book reviews and promoting a love of reading across the school.
**Creative Writing Club**
“Writing is the painting of the voice.” Get your favourite pens ready as we embark on an adventure where your creative dreams can come true. Creative writing club gives all students the chance to develop new vocabulary and enhance their love of writing. Fun and engaging topics ensure students get a chance to develop their own stories.
**Digital Art**
Are you an aspiring artist or graphic designer? Join the digital art club to develop your digital art skills. You will work collaboratively to grow your understanding of digital artwork using the Sketchbook art application.
**Year 3 and Year 4 Choir**
Join the Year 3 and Year 4 choir to develop your inner performer. You will have the chance to enhance your creative talents and grow in confidence as you practise a range of songs throughout the term. This is a great chance to start your stage career early.
**Year 3 and Year 4 Yoga & Mindfulness**
At Sunmarke, well-being and positivity are at the heart of everything we do. In this yoga and mindfulness club, students get the chance to end the day calmly with some relaxing activities that promote mindfulness, well-being and appreciation.
**Creators club**
Do you enjoy being creative? If so, come along to Creators Club where you will have the opportunity to build your self-esteem, resilience, and gain a sense of accomplishment whilst engaging in fun creative arts and crafts projects.
**Maths Stars**
Join the Maths Stars to explore maths in a fun and creative way, whilst also showing off your artistic skills! Each week, you will use your maths knowledge to create a piece of colourful and interesting artwork. You will also have the opportunity to explore some challenging maths problems and investigations.
**Year 3 and Year 4 Board Games**
Strategy and focus are major skills of almost every board game. In this activity, students have the chance to develop these skills in an interactive group setting. Have a favourite board game or one that you think people wouldn’t have played before? Bring it along to Board Games Club and enjoy developing your critical thinking, problem-solving and strategic skills.
**Coding for beginners**
This is a great opportunity to develop your coding skills in a safe and supportive space. The Coding for Beginners club will be able to take students through the key concepts and building blocks of coding in a fun and expressive way.
**Poetry Club**
With a mix of creative writing skills and performance poetry, this club is perfect to build your confidence in both writing and public speaking. During these sessions, students will be sharing fun and engaging activities which develop key literacy skills.
**Zumba Dance**
Get fit and active with the Zumba club; you will have had fun dancing and developing your rhythm. An extremely fun and engaging way for students to express themselves and stay active.
**Woodwork**
Join the woodwork club to gain lots of new key skills. In this club, you will develop your design and technology skills, creating fun and exciting woodwork projects.
**Lego Robotics**
LET’S CODE LEGO! Build your own LEGO creations, improve your coding skills and have lots of fun working within a team. Student will develop their coding and building skills as they tackle challenges and solve problems.
**Mandarin for Beginners Year 3 and Year 4**
Keen to become multi-lingual? Then why not learn Mandarin from a native speaker? This class will give you the chance to learn Mandarin in fun and creative ways, filled with language games and interactive tasks.
#### Year 5 & Year 6
**Stop Motion Animation & Green Screen**
In this exciting club, students will learn how to create short animated films and clips using stop-motion techniques. A great club to develop your creativity and innovation!
**Basketball**
Join the basketball club to develop your inner sportsperson. This club is open to all who would like to build skills in a new sport, or anyone who has an existing passion for basketball. We will see you at the 3-point line.
**Year 5 and Year 6 Yoga & mindfulness**
We are incredibly committed to the well-being of all students. During yoga and mindfulness club, students get the chance to focus on themselves and support their own mental well-being. This has been shown to improve physical and mental health in school-age children. Yoga improves balance and strength.
**Year 5 and Year 6 Book Worms**
Join the Book Worms club to develop an inner love of reading. In this club, you will read both individually and as a group, writing book reviews and promotes a love of reading across the school.
**Year 5 and Year 6 Coding for beginners**
This is a great opportunity to develop your coding skills in a safe and supportive space. The Coding for Beginners club will be able to take students through the key concepts and building blocks of coding in a fun and expressive way.
**watercolour Painting**
Paint like Monet and Vincent Van Gogh! Dive into the history of some of the most famous painters in the world by indulging in the vibrant and translucent nature of watercolour painting. Its quick-drying qualities mean you can take a new masterpiece home with you every single week!
**Sunmarke Newsletter**
This club gives students an opportunity to become journalists and discover and write about all of the exciting things that happen at Sunmarke. We will collaboratively create a Sunmarke newsletter for all to read.
**Year 5 and Year 6 Fun with French**
This club promotes the love of language and the amazing experiences that come along with learning. Students will get the chance to become more confident in French and learn new vocabulary through games and engaging activities.
**Textiles Club**
In this club, you will have the opportunity to learn unique textiles skills such as hand stitching and machine sewing. Your skills will be developed through fun activities and brought together to create your final product.
**Year 5 and Year 6 Board Games**
Strategy and focus are major skills of almost every board game. In this activity, students have the chance to develop these skills in an interactive group setting. Have a favourite board game or one that you think people wouldn’t have played before? Bring it along to Board Games Club and enjoy developing your critical thinking, problem-solving and strategic skills.
**Year 3 – 6 Spanish for Natives**
HOLA! come and join our Spanish for native speakers ECA. Take the opportunity to practise your spoken and written language skills in a fun and collaborative way. We will explore a variety of topics in Spanish, develop an understanding of Spanish and Latin American traditions through a variety of projects and hands on activities.
**Mandarin for Beginners Year 5 and Year 6**
Keen to become multi-lingual? Then why not learn Mandarin from a native speaker? This class will give you the chance to learn Mandarin in fun and creative ways, filled with language games and interactive tasks.
#### Arabic & Islamic
**Arabic Educational Games**
Come and join our Arabic activities club to have fun with educational games that help you to support your Arabic language skills. We will focus on new vocabulary and spoken Arabic.
**Year 5 and Year 6 Arabic Art**
Come and join the Arabic art club to explore the exciting and rich world of Arabic art. We will practise art techniques while also learning about the history and culture surrounding Arabic art. Let’s enjoy doing Arts to discover more fun in Arabic.
**Islamic Culture**
Looking for a fun and educational extracurricular activity? Join our Islamic culture club! This club is open to all students who are interested in learning more about the rich history and traditions of Islam. Through engaging activities and discussions, we explore various aspects of Islamic culture, including art, music, literature, cuisine, and more. Whether you are a practising Muslim or simply interested in learning more about this fascinating culture, our Islamic culture club is the perfect place for you!
**Arabic Reading**
Are you looking to improve your Arabic reading skills? Look no further than our Arabic Reading Club! Our club is open to students of all levels who are interested in enhancing their reading proficiency in Arabic. Through guided reading sessions, group discussions and interactive activities, you’ll have the opportunity to build your vocabulary, improve your pronunciation, and develop your comprehension skills. Our club members come from diverse backgrounds and share a passion for learning the Arabic language and its rich literary tradition. Join us and expand your knowledge of this beautiful language while making new friends and having fun!
**Arabic Arts and Crafts**
Join our Arabic arts and crafts club and explore the beauty of Arabic calligraphy, textile art and more! Our club is open to students of all skill levels who are interested in creating unique and beautiful works of art inspired by the rich cultural heritage of the Arabic-speaking world. Our experienced instructors will guide you through the process of creating your own masterpieces, using a variety of materials and techniques. You’ll have the opportunity to learn about the history and symbolism behind Arabic art and craft traditions, while developing your own creativity and imagination. Our club is a welcoming and inclusive community where you can express yourself, make new friends, and discover the joy of creating something beautiful with your own hands. Join us today and let your creativity soar!
**Emirati Culture club**
Are you fascinated by Emirati culture? Do you want to learn more about the traditions and customs of the United Arab Emirates? Look no further than our Emirati culture club! Our club is open to all students who are interested in exploring the unique and diverse culture of the UAE. Through engaging activities, discussions and events, we delve into various aspects of Emirati culture, including art, music, cuisine and more. You’ll have the opportunity to connect with other students who share your interests, and learn from knowledgeable guest speakers and experts in the field. Whether you are a UAE national or an international student, our Emirati culture club is the perfect place for you to learn about and appreciate the rich cultural heritage of the UAE.

# Governance Committee
[For Parents](https://www.sunmarke.com/for-parents/ "Go to For Parents.") > Governance Committee
# Governance Committee – SUNMAC
The Individuals Who Enrich The School With Their Guidance
The KHDA inspection team has recognised the Governing Body at Sunmarke School as being generously staffed in an outstanding building to enable students to receive high-quality education.
Our Governance Committee comprises the Board of Directors (BOD) and an Advisory Council. The Board includes the owners and the Board of Directors who actively provide strategic direction to the school.
The Advisory Council called “SUNMAC” comprises all stakeholders including parents, teachers and senior leaders This body meets three or four times a year and provides regular inputs.
The Council also supports continuous improvement, and guides the development of the school, holding the leadership accountable for the quality of education delivered.
Sub-committees have also been created to enable parents and leaders to evaluate key aspects of delivery in more detail with focus on Inclusion, Child Protection, Safeguarding, Health & Safety, Learning and Teaching, Attainment and Progress, Leadership and Professional Development.
## Advisory Council – SUNMAC

# Our Approach
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Nursery](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/nursery/ "Go to Nursery.") > Our Approach
# Our Approach
Dubai’s Most Loved Early Childhood Centre - Where Each Day Brings A New Learning Adventure
![jins-new-theme-logo-web1](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg2MTozNDY4-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMzE1IDEzNSIgd2lkdGg9IjMxNSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMzUiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
Part of the award-winning group of Fortes Education Schools and located within Sunmarke’s campus, Jumeirah International Nurseries (JINS) enables a smooth and seamless transition into the Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) at Sunmarke School.
With over 40 years of Early Years excellence, JINS Early Childhood Centre provides an inspiring British education that nurtures the hearts and minds of children aged 45+ days old and sparks infinite potential in all our little learners.
# Nursery School in Dubai
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg2NToxMjI4-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
#### Areas of Learning
JINS’ unique educational framework blends the World-Class British EYFS Curriculum with Reggio-Emilia, The Curiosity Approach and Montessori principles to bring hands-on learning experiences to life. Our curriculum is further enriched with STEAM and Positive Education to set the stage for an exciting journey of discovery.
Our Early Years Experts create authentic and meaningful learning experiences that embrace the individuality of each child, allowing them to progress and flourish based on their unique interests, abilities and aptitude.
These seven areas of learning are essential for your child’s healthy development and form the core framework of our educational programmes:
**3 Core Areas of Learning**
  * Physical Development
  * Communication and Language
  * Personal, Social and Emotional Development


**4 Specific Areas of Learning**
  * Literacy
  * Mathematics
  * Understanding of the World
  * Expressive Arts and Design


#### Extraordinary Indoor & Outdoor Spaces
With 9 vibrant branches in Dubai, JINS provides rich learning environments that ignite children’s imagination and stimulate endless moments of discovery and exploration.
Our nature-inspired Early Childhood Centre in the **heart of the JVT community** offers stimulating and engaging spaces where every detail is crafted to inspire and nurture young minds.
Children at JINS Sunmarke, JVT, have access to large, Reggio-inspired classrooms and a shared courtyard to which all classroom doors open, making it a welcoming space where children explore through sensory and physical play whilst also forging a strong connection with nature through our outdoor mud kitchen and garden areas.
![JINS Nursery](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg5NToxNDU=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTAyMSA2ODEiIHdpZHRoPSIxMDIxIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjY4MSIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)

#### Learning Through Play
Our passionate educators create joy-filled learning experiences where children ‘learn through play’ and hands-on activities that enhance their understanding and comprehension of the world around them. Our open-ended Reggio-inspired learning resources encourage creativity and problem-solving and enable our children to become confident and independent little learners.
![JINS](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=MjkzODoxMzc=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNzcwIDUxMyIgd2lkdGg9Ijc3MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MTMiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
![JINS](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjk0NzoxMzc=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNzc3IDUxOCIgd2lkdGg9Ijc3NyIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MTgiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
#### Outstanding Care
The wellbeing of our children and families is at the heart of what we do. We’re committed to exceeding and maintaining the highest international standards of quality and safety in early childhood education.
We are proud holders of the**ISO 9001 Certification (Quality Systems)** and are the only Nursery in the UAE with the **ISO 21001 (Education Systems) Certification.**
At JINS, we follow the below rigorous health and safety procedures at our Early Childhood Centres:
  * Clean, fresh, and sanitised learning environments
  * Child-friendly changing facilities and toilets located within the classroom
  * Inculcating children with practices of good hygiene throughout the day
  * A full-time nurse on site
  * Doctor visits twice a week
  * Stringent security measures with identifiable lanyards for all adults (parents/visitors/staff) to wear at all times
  * Security gates surrounding the nursery externally and internally
  * All families are invited for a staggered entry week allowing a smooth and steady start for children


#### Transition to Big School
At JINS JVT, we help our children develop and secure skills which will be essential for a smooth transition into EYFS at Sunmarke School at the age of 3.
We support our young learners as they develop pre-writing and literacy skills and build their knowledge of important mathematical concepts through play and exploration. Children learn how to identify and write their own names and are introduced to the Letters and Sounds and Accelerated Maths Programme.
On a day-to-day basis, the children are encouraged to be independent, think critically, ask questions, and use their imagination as they learn and explore together.
In addition to this, our children regularly go across and experience the learning spaces in EYFS at Sunmarke School in the Summer Term, allowing them to acclimatise to new environments, practitioners, peers, routines, and levels of expectation. During these ‘Transition Days’ children experience the Forest Schools in EYFS at both schools, visit the libraries and play spaces, and enjoy specialised lessons such as Physical Education and Music.
#### The JINS Family Club
We believe that Early Childhood Centres are more important than ever before. As well as being crucial years for your child’s development and preparation for life in the modern world, it’s also a place for families to come together and feel supported.
Our parents can become a member of the JINS Family Club where they’ll have access to expert speakers and interactive workshops aimed at supporting you on your parenting journey.
Follow the JINS Family Club Instagram Page [**here.**](https://www.instagram.com/jins_familyclub/)
## JINS Awards & Recognitions
JINS’ unique approach to early years education, innovative facilities and best teaching practices has gained recognition from parents and other distinguished platforms in the region.
**Some of our awards and recognitions include:**
## Gulf News and Being She Lifetime Achievement Award – Mrs. Mankani, Founder of JINS
![Mrs Mankani](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjk4MDoxNTE=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTA4MCAxMDgwIiB3aWR0aD0iMTA4MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMDgwIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)
Our visionary and esteemed Founder, Mrs. Shakuntala Mankani, has been honoured with the Lifetime Achievement Award at the prestigious Gulf News and Being She Excellence Awards 2024. This accolade was presented by Her Excellency Shaikha Lubna Bint Khalid Al Qasimi, a prominent Emirati politician and a respected member of the Sharjah royal family. This recognition celebrates Mrs. Mankani’s extraordinary contributions to the education sector and her unwavering commitment to academic excellence and innovation.
To read more about the Being She Awards in association with Gulf News and this inspiring event, please view the feature article [**here.**](https://gulfnews.com/uae/46-women-shine-bright-at-2024-excellence-awards-1.1718020723861)
## Mother, Baby & Child Awards 2021-22 – Gold Winners
We are proud to announce that Jumeirah International Nurseries won the **MBC Nursery of the Year 2021-22 Award**. We thank all the JINS families who have showered us with so much love, our JINS team for their outstanding commitment, and most of all our amazing children for making each moment spent with them so special.
![MBC2](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjk4NToxNTI=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMzAwIDMwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjMwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIzMDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
## The Bayut Awards
We were nominated as the Best Nursery Chain in Dubai at the prestigious Bayut Awards 2021. These awards pay tribute to the vision of HH Sheikh Mohammed Bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Vice President of UAE and Ruler of Dubai, towards making Dubai the happiest city in the world.
## The Readers Choice Award
Jumeirah International Nurseries were proud winners of the Best Nursery 2018 Award by the Russian Emirates, Family and Kids Magazine.
## Edarabia – Parents Choice
Jumeirah International Nurseries received the ‘Parents Choice’ 2017 Award by Edarabia.com.
![parents-choice-badge-2019](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjk5MjoxOTQ=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMjAwIDIwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjIwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyMDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)

# Transport Services
[For Parents](https://www.sunmarke.com/for-parents/ "Go to For Parents.") > Transport Services
# Transport Services
Ensuring Your Child Reaches School Safely 
[![Mybus-logo](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=MjgzNTozMzY1-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNTQ3MyAxNDA1IiB3aWR0aD0iNTQ3MyIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxNDA1IiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)](https://mybus.co/)
Sunmarke uses an independent third party bus operator **“My Bus Transport Services LLC”** to make the school run as easy as possible.
“My Bus” operates an air-conditioned and efficient transport service from various parts of Dubai to the school. Parents may avail of this service at their own discretion and cost.
![Transport Service](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0NDoxNDM=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjAwIDMyMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIzMjAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
![sms-Transport](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MzoxNTE=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjAwIDMyMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIzMjAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
Transport fees are paid in advance prior to the beginning of every term on the following dates:
**Term 1:** 1st August  
**Term 2:** 15th November  
**Term 3:** 15th February
The transport service, routes and fees are governed by the third party transport company’s rules and regulations, which are subject to change at any time. All payments must be made to the transport company’s name and account as under:
## Bus Service Booking Procedure
[Click here](https://www.sunmarke.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/Bus-Service-Booking-Procedure.pdf) to view our bus service booking procedure
#### "My Bus" Account Details
**Account Name:** My Bus Transport Services LLC  
**Account Number:** 012943071037  
**Bank:** National Bank of Fujairah  
**Branch:** Jebel Ali  
**Swift:** NBFUAEAFDXB  
**Iban Number:** AE020380000012943071037  
**Remarks:** Student Name / Year Group / Term (1,2,3) / Transport Fees  
**Please email proof of payment of the transport fees to:** transportpayments@forteseducation.com
#### Bus Routes  
| Bus  | Routes  |

# Enriched Learning
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Nursery](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/nursery/ "Go to Nursery.") > Enriched Learning
# Enriched Learning
Rich Experiences to Give Your Child’s Educational Journey a Head-start.
![](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=MjgzNTozODUx-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMzAwIDEyNyIgd2lkdGg9IjMwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMjciIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)  
At JINS, every child that joins us goes on an individual learning journey to fulfill their potential. We take a holistic approach and support our children with enriching learning opportunities that take them beyond academics, including classes in Arabic, French, Arabic, Russian, Gymnastics and Ballet.
## Activities and Clubs
  * Our Specialist Arabic and French classes are taught in a fun and interactive manner on a weekly basis
  * Music and Dance classes help our children’s physical development through the use of music and movement
  * We work with many 3rd Party Providers to offer extensive extracurricular activities such as Gymnastics, Ballet, Drama, Gardening, Football, Judo, Tennis, STEAM and Robotics, plus a Healthy Eating Programme with the provider ‘Baby Eats’


![](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0OToxMzU=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjAwIDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)

![](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg2NzoxMzU=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjAwIDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
![](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg3NjoxMzU=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjAwIDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
## Aftercare Programme: The JINS Learning Hub
The JINS Learning Hub is an afternoon enrichment programme offering a variety of fun and educational activities for children daily from 1:00 pm to 6:00 pm. Key features include:
  * A play-based environment and custom-designed tasks to enhance children’s learning
  * Myriad indoor and outdoor facilities including climbing frames, sandpits and discovery areas
  * Art and craft projects to help students explore their creativity
  * Problem-solving in mathematics and construction areas
  * Reading areas for rest and recreation
  * Fully trained staff at all clubs to support children and scaffold their learning

# Our Approach
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Nursery](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/nursery/ "Go to Nursery.") > Our Approach
# Our Approach
Dubai’s Most Loved Early Childhood Centre - Where Each Day Brings A New Learning Adventure
![jins-new-theme-logo-web1](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg2MTozNDY4-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMzE1IDEzNSIgd2lkdGg9IjMxNSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMzUiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
Part of the award-winning group of Fortes Education Schools and located within Sunmarke’s campus, Jumeirah International Nurseries (JINS) enables a smooth and seamless transition into the Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) at Sunmarke School.
With over 40 years of Early Years excellence, JINS Early Childhood Centre provides an inspiring British education that nurtures the hearts and minds of children aged 45+ days old and sparks infinite potential in all our little learners.
# Nursery School in Dubai
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg2NToxMjI4-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
#### Areas of Learning
JINS’ unique educational framework blends the World-Class British EYFS Curriculum with Reggio-Emilia, The Curiosity Approach and Montessori principles to bring hands-on learning experiences to life. Our curriculum is further enriched with STEAM and Positive Education to set the stage for an exciting journey of discovery.
Our Early Years Experts create authentic and meaningful learning experiences that embrace the individuality of each child, allowing them to progress and flourish based on their unique interests, abilities and aptitude.
These seven areas of learning are essential for your child’s healthy development and form the core framework of our educational programmes:
**3 Core Areas of Learning**
  * Physical Development
  * Communication and Language
  * Personal, Social and Emotional Development


**4 Specific Areas of Learning**
  * Literacy
  * Mathematics
  * Understanding of the World
  * Expressive Arts and Design


#### Extraordinary Indoor & Outdoor Spaces
With 9 vibrant branches in Dubai, JINS provides rich learning environments that ignite children’s imagination and stimulate endless moments of discovery and exploration.
Our nature-inspired Early Childhood Centre in the **heart of the JVT community** offers stimulating and engaging spaces where every detail is crafted to inspire and nurture young minds.
Children at JINS Sunmarke, JVT, have access to large, Reggio-inspired classrooms and a shared courtyard to which all classroom doors open, making it a welcoming space where children explore through sensory and physical play whilst also forging a strong connection with nature through our outdoor mud kitchen and garden areas.
![JINS Nursery](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg5NToxNDU=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTAyMSA2ODEiIHdpZHRoPSIxMDIxIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjY4MSIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)

#### Learning Through Play
Our passionate educators create joy-filled learning experiences where children ‘learn through play’ and hands-on activities that enhance their understanding and comprehension of the world around them. Our open-ended Reggio-inspired learning resources encourage creativity and problem-solving and enable our children to become confident and independent little learners.
![JINS](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=MjkzODoxMzc=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNzcwIDUxMyIgd2lkdGg9Ijc3MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MTMiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
![JINS](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjk0NzoxMzc=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNzc3IDUxOCIgd2lkdGg9Ijc3NyIgaGVpZ2h0PSI1MTgiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
#### Outstanding Care
The wellbeing of our children and families is at the heart of what we do. We’re committed to exceeding and maintaining the highest international standards of quality and safety in early childhood education.
We are proud holders of the**ISO 9001 Certification (Quality Systems)** and are the only Nursery in the UAE with the **ISO 21001 (Education Systems) Certification.**
At JINS, we follow the below rigorous health and safety procedures at our Early Childhood Centres:
  * Clean, fresh, and sanitised learning environments
  * Child-friendly changing facilities and toilets located within the classroom
  * Inculcating children with practices of good hygiene throughout the day
  * A full-time nurse on site
  * Doctor visits twice a week
  * Stringent security measures with identifiable lanyards for all adults (parents/visitors/staff) to wear at all times
  * Security gates surrounding the nursery externally and internally
  * All families are invited for a staggered entry week allowing a smooth and steady start for children


#### Transition to Big School
At JINS JVT, we help our children develop and secure skills which will be essential for a smooth transition into EYFS at Sunmarke School at the age of 3.
We support our young learners as they develop pre-writing and literacy skills and build their knowledge of important mathematical concepts through play and exploration. Children learn how to identify and write their own names and are introduced to the Letters and Sounds and Accelerated Maths Programme.
On a day-to-day basis, the children are encouraged to be independent, think critically, ask questions, and use their imagination as they learn and explore together.
In addition to this, our children regularly go across and experience the learning spaces in EYFS at Sunmarke School in the Summer Term, allowing them to acclimatise to new environments, practitioners, peers, routines, and levels of expectation. During these ‘Transition Days’ children experience the Forest Schools in EYFS at both schools, visit the libraries and play spaces, and enjoy specialised lessons such as Physical Education and Music.
#### The JINS Family Club
We believe that Early Childhood Centres are more important than ever before. As well as being crucial years for your child’s development and preparation for life in the modern world, it’s also a place for families to come together and feel supported.
Our parents can become a member of the JINS Family Club where they’ll have access to expert speakers and interactive workshops aimed at supporting you on your parenting journey.
Follow the JINS Family Club Instagram Page [**here.**](https://www.instagram.com/jins_familyclub/)
## JINS Awards & Recognitions
JINS’ unique approach to early years education, innovative facilities and best teaching practices has gained recognition from parents and other distinguished platforms in the region.
**Some of our awards and recognitions include:**
## Gulf News and Being She Lifetime Achievement Award – Mrs. Mankani, Founder of JINS
![Mrs Mankani](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjk4MDoxNTE=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTA4MCAxMDgwIiB3aWR0aD0iMTA4MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMDgwIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)
Our visionary and esteemed Founder, Mrs. Shakuntala Mankani, has been honoured with the Lifetime Achievement Award at the prestigious Gulf News and Being She Excellence Awards 2024. This accolade was presented by Her Excellency Shaikha Lubna Bint Khalid Al Qasimi, a prominent Emirati politician and a respected member of the Sharjah royal family. This recognition celebrates Mrs. Mankani’s extraordinary contributions to the education sector and her unwavering commitment to academic excellence and innovation.
To read more about the Being She Awards in association with Gulf News and this inspiring event, please view the feature article [**here.**](https://gulfnews.com/uae/46-women-shine-bright-at-2024-excellence-awards-1.1718020723861)
## Mother, Baby & Child Awards 2021-22 – Gold Winners
We are proud to announce that Jumeirah International Nurseries won the **MBC Nursery of the Year 2021-22 Award**. We thank all the JINS families who have showered us with so much love, our JINS team for their outstanding commitment, and most of all our amazing children for making each moment spent with them so special.
![MBC2](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjk4NToxNTI=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMzAwIDMwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjMwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIzMDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
## The Bayut Awards
We were nominated as the Best Nursery Chain in Dubai at the prestigious Bayut Awards 2021. These awards pay tribute to the vision of HH Sheikh Mohammed Bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Vice President of UAE and Ruler of Dubai, towards making Dubai the happiest city in the world.
## The Readers Choice Award
Jumeirah International Nurseries were proud winners of the Best Nursery 2018 Award by the Russian Emirates, Family and Kids Magazine.
## Edarabia – Parents Choice
Jumeirah International Nurseries received the ‘Parents Choice’ 2017 Award by Edarabia.com.
![parents-choice-badge-2019](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjk5MjoxOTQ=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMjAwIDIwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjIwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyMDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)

# Parent Engagement
[For Parents](https://www.sunmarke.com/for-parents/ "Go to For Parents.") > Parent Engagement
# Parent Engagement
Making Parents An Integral Part Of Their Child's Educational Journey
In tune with the traditions adopted by the Fortes Education Group, Sunmarke emphasises a strong Parent Engagement Programme with parents being as involved in our community and aspects of school life as their children.
Parents participate in their child’s education by attending assemblies, Parent’s Evenings, helping out with reading clubs and joining our popular monthly Wellness Wednesdays sessions for useful parenting and lifestyle advice by experts.
## The Parents Association – FOSS
The Friends of Sunmarke School (FOSS) plays a central role, organising various community events throughout the year. Some calendar favourites include the Movie Night, World Culture Day, Cup Cake Sales, Mother’s Day, and Dance-A-Thon to name just a few.
The Association also provides vital support to parents and families joining the school or those who have just moved to Dubai. The FOSS coffee mornings and the FOSS ‘Healthy Hub’ are great ways to make new friends and socialise.
If you would like to know more about the activities and events organised by FOSS or would like to join this parent body, please contact admin@forteseducation.com
