## Wellness Wednesday
Held once a month, Wellness Wednesday sessions allow parents to listen to a variety of specialist speakers and experts on important topics, be it the most recent research in child development or the best practices in Positive Education.
Check out our video recordings to learn how to manage stress and anxiety, build resilience, practice cyber safety, and more!
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=MjkxMjo2ODg=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
[Click here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S-weLEYFWwE&list=PLA3UgpUvdtrJnJ1XqVl3sgdcw9EZrlrsC) to view our “Wellness Wednesday” events.

# Food
[For Parents](https://www.sunmarke.com/for-parents/ "Go to For Parents.") > Food
# Dining & Catering
Healthy, Wholesome And Nutritious Meals For Your Child
We understand the important role that health and nutrition play in a student’s overall wellbeing and therefore, encourage healthy eating habits from an early age. Towards this, we have partnered with Mastercook the catering experts, to tailor-make our dining menus and meal programmes.
![Food](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0MjoxNDA=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTkyMCAxMjgwIiB3aWR0aD0iMTkyMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMjgwIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)
![Food](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MToxNDA=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTkyMCAxMjgwIiB3aWR0aD0iMTkyMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMjgwIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)
We have a bright and spacious cafeteria where students can enjoy healthy food options, socialise, and deepen their friendships. They can opt for pre-packaged food in environment-friendly packs as well. We also provide Grab & Go snacks for them.
## Menu and Pricing:
The [Mastercook menu](https://www.sunmarke.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/2-Week-Master-menus-2023-without-beef-.pdf) offers a selection of nutritious, additive-free and preservative-free school meals. With options from various cuisines, the lunch menu is priced at AED 24.25 Keeping dietary restrictions in mind, the nut-free menu has been crafted with care and quality.
Parents of EYFS and Primary students can easily place their orders via the provided link, with lunch delivered directly to their child’s classroom.
<http://form.jotform.com/Mastercook/Sunmarke-stripe>
Secondary students can savor their lunch in the school canteen. Payments can be made using credit cards, Apple Pay or Google Pay
**Health & Safety**
Mastercook implements robust safety measures in their kitchens, including the use of PPE kits and a Safety Ambassador to oversee quality and hygiene. For additional information, please consult our [**FAQs** ](https://www.sunmarke.com/faqs/)or reach out to:

# Sports & PE
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Primary](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/primary/ "Go to Primary.") > Sports & PE
# Sports & PE
‘Teamwork makes the dream work!’ is the motto of our sporting heroes at Sunmarke Primary
At Sunmarke School, our world-class PE department offers a diverse range of squads and teams, providing students with ample opportunities to excel in various sports. Whether your child aspires to be a top footballer or a champion swimmer, Sunmarke nurtures their ambitions and helps turn their dreams into reality. Our commitment to sporting excellence is evident in our recent achievements. In March 2024, our primary students clinched first place overall at the prestigious U11 British Schools in the Middle East (BSME) Games. This remarkable feat included victories in girls’ football, athletics, and boys’ basketball, highlighting the dedication and talent of our young athletes.
For students who enjoy sports and fitness but prefer a non-competitive environment, we offer a variety of clubs and activities designed to enhance their physical well-being. Our passionate and dedicated PE staff work diligently to build each child’s stamina and resilience, ensuring they perform at their highest level. With state-of-the-art facilities, Sunmarke provides an environment where every student can thrive.

# Our Approach

# Secondary School in Dubai
# Our Approach
Our Secondary school is alive with activity from dawn until dusk – as students pursue their ambitions in the classroom and beyond.
![Secondary_Our-Approach_1](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0MjoxNzM=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjE1IDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYxNSIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
![Secondary_Our-Approach_2](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MToxNzM=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjE1IDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYxNSIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
## Seamless Transition
Sunmarke Secondary School ensures that students have a smooth transition from Primary to Secondary school to succeed in the next phase of their academic journey.
To make the experience less daunting, our staff works closely with families. Our transition programme from Primary to Secondary school ensures that they start Year 7 full of excitement and with minimal nervousness.
This section contains important and up-to-date information about our transition programme. If you have any questions please do not hesitate to contact Ms Young (Deputy Head – Student Opportunity) or Mr Matthews (Head of Year 7).
## Transitioning Into Secondary
We strive to do our utmost to ensure that the transition from Year 6 to Year 7 is as smooth and welcoming as possible.
#### **Days for Your Diary**
**Upcoming activities:**
  * Secondary pastoral leaders visit Year 6 children in their classrooms to hold informal Q&A sessions.
  * Parent Coffee Mornings are held in Term Two and Three to learn more about the Secondary School and how to support your children with the transition process. This is an opportunity to meet key members of staff such as the Secondary Leadership Team.
  * During the summer term, children will have the opportunity to experience Secondary Taster lessons. This will give them the chance to meet some of our secondary school staff and experience this part of the school building.


More information and timings for the above events will be available soon via VLE.
### Approach To Learning In Secondary
Our approach encourages students to build the skills and learning habits to become independent thinkers – preparing them for life way beyond Year 13. 
#### Thinking Skills
At Sunmarke through our ‘Positive Education’ inclusive approach, students are enabled to flourish in a safe, happy and nurturing learning environment. They are stretched and challenged to ask and develop their own questions to harness their potential to the maximum.
Opportunities are provided through numerous activities undertaken in the classroom, on the sports field, on stage, through educational visits abroad, organising charity events, or representing the school as outstanding ambassadors.
#### Leadership
There are numerous opportunities for leadership from informal roles within the classroom to formal roles outside of the classroom e.g. Prefects, School and Community Ambassadors.
Students can aspire to leadership roles within and outside their year groups. These roles are designed to promote excellence while enabling student leaders to play a role in the development of the school and community.
The Student Hub within the Secondary School is a dedicated space for secondary students to meet with their Student Leadership Team. At Sunmarke we believe in student voice and the sharing of ideas. The Hub provides a platform for every student to be heard and to interact with their Student Leaders.
#### Digital Learning
Sunmarke students are adept at using technology through class assignment and quizzes, home learning, submission and assessments, online collaboration and communication between students and teachers, tracking progress and behaviour and access to school resources, library books and information.
We promote and sustain a culture that encourages the integration of digital technologies into all areas of the curriculum. Students engage and collaborate with their peers using a variety of digital environments and tools.
Cyber-safety is an integral part of how the school community works with everyone being aware of their digital responsibilities, modelling ethical and appropriate use of digital resources.
Please view our Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) [here](https://www.sunmarke.com/for-parents/policies/).
#### Social Responsibility
Our principled, creative and innovative learning environment encourages students to have a responsibility ‘for’ and ‘towards’ the community. They learn to be solution-focused, discuss controversial topics, share their opinions on what they learn, ask questions and engage in dialogue.
#### Environment and Sustainability
Sustainability is a key agenda at Sunmarke School. We believe that we have a shared responsibility as citizens of the world to choose sustainable practices and we empower our students to do the same.
Below are just some of the ways we incorporate the environment and sustainability within our subjects.
**Media** – Sunmarke’s Media students focus on projects related to sustainable transportation, the effects of pollution and draw upon the beauty of nature and landscapes when building their portfolios.
**Economics** – Our Business and Economics courses cover topics such as economic growth vs development, effects of trade and globalisation, the impact of deforestation etc. Students are encouraged to research policies to solve urgent environmental issues.
**The Sciences –** A Level and IB Biology – Both courses include the study of ecosystems, ecology and biodiversity. Secondary students also look into the sustainability of using earth’s resources and assess the impact of extracting, using and recycling.
#### Global Outlook
Our excellent, holistic education and team of dedicated teachers place values like honesty, responsibility, tolerance and respect for the rights of others irrespective of nationality, gender, culture or religion at the foundation of learning.
This provides students the skills and knowledge to meet the challenges of the future. Our high-quality education and multi-pathway model with bespoke career paths guide them to find the most appropriate route to study their favourite subjects at the university of their choice.

# Our Approach
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Sixth Form](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/sixth-form/ "Go to Sixth Form.") > Our Approach
# Sixth Form School in Dubai
# Our Approach
Achieving Outstanding Results With The Right Balance of Support, Inspiration And Challenge
Sunmarke’s Sixth Form is imbued with the Positive Education approach, our dynamic and innovative Sixth Form curriculum is designed to meet the needs, talents and interest of our students, making them future-ready. Through our different pathways, we encourage students to ask questions about the world around them and think from multiple perspectives.
We are unique in offering A-Levels, BTECs, and being an IB World School, we also offer the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme. Whether students choose the A-Level, BTEC; or the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme pathway, we inspire purpose and drive passion.
Here at Sunmarke we care about the journey that students go on and the skills they develop along the way. Our students are not defined by grades, they are a collection of skills and interests that show their passion, and we want them to strive to be better learners to best prepare them for the path they choose after they graduate. Students will develop these skills through sessions run in form time on developing as a learner and preparing for higher education. While attending sessions for the latter, students will be exposed to resources that will help them explore their passions through reading, videos and courses outside of the curriculum that are now available online at the click of a button.
Outside of the form room is where you will differ in terms of skills developed. The International Baccalaureate Programmes places more of an emphasis in developing a variety of skills due to the diverse subjects students have to take whereas A-Levels allow students to delve deeper into a smaller selection of subjects. Different still are BTEC qualifications which have no exams and will, therefore, develop your investigation and research skills as you look to answer your briefs.
![Sunmarke Sixth Form](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0NjoxNjU=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjAwIDMyMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIzMjAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
![Sixth-Form_Approach1](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1NToxNjU=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjIwIDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYyMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
#### Leadership
All students at Sunmarke’s Sixth Form are leaders and role models and they embrace this responsibility fully. These young leaders play a crucial role in the growth of the school and the community. 
Within their academics, students are provided options to lead their own learning such as through the Extended Project Qualification (EPQ), available to A Level and BTEC students or through various pieces of coursework, such as the Extended Essay and Reflective Project on the International Baccalaureate pathways.
There are several opportunities for students to assume leadership roles, form their own extra-curricular activities and represent the school as Senior Prefects, Senior Student Leadership Team, House Captains and Subject Ambassadors. 
#### Thinking Skills
Preparing students to flourish in an environment that encourages critical and independent thinking, Sunmarke’s Sixth Form is imbued with the Positive Education approach. Students are trained through the Theory of Knowledge (TOK) curriculum that motivates them to ask questions about the world around them and think from multiple perspectives.
#### Digital Learning
Sixth Form students model the use and integration of digital technologies throughout their curriculum. Many digital tools are available such as Questia School, an online library and UniFrog, a platform for search, shortlist and applying for university courses.
Sixth Form Students are taught to be at ease with technology, but also encouraged to question its validity, reliability and responsibility in a modern world. Media literacy is given a lot of importance at Sunmarke as students prepare to navigate the world beyond the school.
Please view our Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy [here](https://www.sunmarke.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/E-Safety-BYOD-Policy_SMS-v1.3-1.pdf).
#### Social Responsibility
Sunmarke Sixth Formers take the lead in service, learning and providing solutions while developing collaboration, leadership and social skills.
There are structured programmes for students to follow such as CAS (Creativity, Activity, Service) and Service Learning that lists a set of objectives to be used as guidance.
These are ‘need and solution-based’ programmes where students can choose to give service to their peers, the school, the local community and even make a global difference. These programmes make them understand the difference they can make to the world while developing emotional intelligence.
#### Global Outlook
As an international school training students to excel globally, we foster an ethos of honesty, responsibility, tolerance and respect for others within the academic and Positive Education curriculum.
Sixth Form students take a leading role in celebrations such as International Day by sharing their own cultures and traditions with the rest of the community. As a multi-pathway school, we aim to make our students global citizens. 
#### Sixth Form Options Booklet
We have designed the below booklet to inform and support our students on the multiple pathways available to them. We are unique in offering A Levels, BTECs, and being an IB world school, we also offer the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme and the International Baccalaureate Career Related Programme. It is therefore, important to understand each one to make an informed decision.
**Sunmarke parents can view the PDF version of the Sixth Form Options Booklet from our parent communication portal, VLE.

# Student Enrichment
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Sixth Form](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/sixth-form/ "Go to Sixth Form.") > Student Enrichment
# Student Enrichment
A range of activities to inspire students to explore their interests and talents 
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=MjgzNTo0MTgz-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
Organising visits from the prestigious Oxford Union and being a host school for the world-renowned World Scholars Cup and TEDx talks, external speakers at Think & Thrive sessions, ensures that everyone has a voice at Sunmarke. 
#### Civitas
To know more [click here](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/sixth-form/civitas/)
![Civitas-Tile](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=MjgzODoxNzA=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNDk0IDE1MiIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5NCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxNTIiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
#### Trips
Our trips this year were focused team building and building skills such as communication, problem solving, and teamwork.
  * Duke of Edinburgh Programme
  * Trip to New York


![Trip to Newyork](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0NDoxNjM=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMjU2MCAxNzA3IiB3aWR0aD0iMjU2MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxNzA3IiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)
#### Public Speaking
  * Debates during life skills lesson
  * Think & thrive – hosting (MC)
  * MUN
  * Interviews – one-to-one with Careers Counsellor to prepare for real-life situations


![Think & Thrive - Heston](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MToxODU=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMjU2MCAxNzA5IiB3aWR0aD0iMjU2MCIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxNzA5IiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)
#### Leadership
Opportunities for students to develop leadership through various positions, such as
  * Civitas Societies
  * Civitas Branches
  * Student Leadership Team
  * TedXSunmarke


![TedXSunmarke](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1OToxNjQ=-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNjAwIDQwMCIgd2lkdGg9IjYwMCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MDAiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)
#### Drop Down Day Y13
In preparation for university, students were given sessions on
  * Cooking
  * Ironing
  * Sewing

# Civitas
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Sixth Form](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/sixth-form/ "Go to Sixth Form.") > Civitas
# CIVITAS
A student leadership programme that aims to builds leadership skills through various community initiatives 
_Civitas is a Latin term for a body of cives or citizens united by a common law or purpose_
## Our Mission
Our mission is to develop global leaders with outstanding citizenship.
## Our Philosophy
We believe that leadership, empathy and community can be nurtured in each student through various community outreach initiatives.
We believe each student possesses unique strengths and capabilities and it is our responsibility to provide opportunities for personal growth and empowerment by enabling individuals to make meaningful contributions to Civitas societies.
## Our Purpose
Civitas provides an exciting platform and opportunity for Sixth Form students to lead initiatives developing their leadership and organizational skills as well as knowledge.
Each initiative or project has the dual purpose of student enrichment and community outreach developing engaged global citizens.

#### Meet the CIVITAS Team
  * Introducing the Head of Civitas Societies!
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0NjoxNjg4-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
Meet the extraordinary leader at Civitas, overseeing our 7 vibrant societies, including Medicine and Law. Get ready for exciting updates as our society heads take the stage to share their insights and initiatives. Stay tuned on our socials for more from our inspiring leaders!
  * Introducing our Math Tutoring Leader!
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg0ODoxMzE1-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
Meet Muhammad, the exceptional leader of our Math tutoring initiative within Civitas, our inspiring programme for student development, collaboration, and enrichment. Muhammad leads our dedicated volunteers from Secondary and Sixth Form as they support younger students in solving math problems. Stay tuned to learn more about their invaluable contributions!
  * Introducing leader of our Student Enrichment Branch!
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MDoxMzMw-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
Here’s introducing Kynan, the dynamic leader of our Student Enrichment Branch of the Civitas Programme, an exciting initiative for student growth and development. The goal is to foster a vibrant community where children support each other and work together in an academic setting. Stay tuned to learn more about the programme, including the ‘Student Academic Tutoring’ aspect, where senior volunteers provide valuable support to lower secondary students.
  * Introducing Co-Leaders of the Science Society!
![YouTube player](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Mjg1MjoxMzI0-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMTI4MCA3MjAiIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgeG1sbnM9Imh0dHA6Ly93d3cudzMub3JnLzIwMDAvc3ZnIj48L3N2Zz4=)
Introducing Civitas’ Co-Leaders of the Science Society – a passionate community of students devoted to all things Science! Our leaders will empower younger students through exciting activities and events, enriching their knowledge and igniting their passion for Science.

# Our Approach
[Learning](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/ "Go to Learning.") > [Sixth Form](https://www.sunmarke.com/learning/sixth-form/ "Go to Sixth Form.") > Our Approach
# Sixth Form School in Dubai
# Our Approach
Achieving Outstanding Results With The Right Balance of Support, Inspiration And Challenge
Sunmarke’s Sixth Form is imbued with the Positive Education approach, our dynamic and innovative Sixth Form curriculum is designed to meet the needs, talents and interest of our students, making them future-ready. Through our different pathways, we encourage students to ask questions about the world around them and think from multiple perspectives.
We are unique in offering A-Levels, BTECs, and being an IB World School, we also offer the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme. Whether students choose the A-Level, BTEC; or the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme pathway, we inspire purpose and drive passion.
Here at Sunmarke we care about the journey that students go on and the skills they develop along the way. Our students are not defined by grades, they are a collection of skills and interests that show their passion, and we want them to strive to be better learners to best prepare them for the path they choose after they graduate. Students will develop these skills through sessions run in form time on developing as a learner and preparing for higher education. While attending sessions for the latter, students will be exposed to resources that will help them explore their passions through reading, videos and courses outside of the curriculum that are now available online at the click of a button.
Outside of the form room is where you will differ in terms of skills developed. The International Baccalaureate Programmes places more of an emphasis in developing a variety of skills due to the diverse subjects students have to take whereas A-Levels allow students to delve deeper into a smaller selection of subjects. Different still are BTEC qualifications which have no exams and will, therefore, develop your investigation and research skills as you look to answer your briefs.

#### Leadership
All students at Sunmarke’s Sixth Form are leaders and role models and they embrace this responsibility fully. These young leaders play a crucial role in the growth of the school and the community. 
Within their academics, students are provided options to lead their own learning such as through the Extended Project Qualification (EPQ), available to A Level and BTEC students or through various pieces of coursework, such as the Extended Essay and Reflective Project on the International Baccalaureate pathways.
There are several opportunities for students to assume leadership roles, form their own extra-curricular activities and represent the school as Senior Prefects, Senior Student Leadership Team, House Captains and Subject Ambassadors. 
#### Thinking Skills
Preparing students to flourish in an environment that encourages critical and independent thinking, Sunmarke’s Sixth Form is imbued with the Positive Education approach. Students are trained through the Theory of Knowledge (TOK) curriculum that motivates them to ask questions about the world around them and think from multiple perspectives.
#### Digital Learning
Sixth Form students model the use and integration of digital technologies throughout their curriculum. Many digital tools are available such as Questia School, an online library and UniFrog, a platform for search, shortlist and applying for university courses.
Sixth Form Students are taught to be at ease with technology, but also encouraged to question its validity, reliability and responsibility in a modern world. Media literacy is given a lot of importance at Sunmarke as students prepare to navigate the world beyond the school.
Please view our Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy [here](https://www.sunmarke.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/E-Safety-BYOD-Policy_SMS-v1.3-1.pdf).
#### Social Responsibility
Sunmarke Sixth Formers take the lead in service, learning and providing solutions while developing collaboration, leadership and social skills.
There are structured programmes for students to follow such as CAS (Creativity, Activity, Service) and Service Learning that lists a set of objectives to be used as guidance.
These are ‘need and solution-based’ programmes where students can choose to give service to their peers, the school, the local community and even make a global difference. These programmes make them understand the difference they can make to the world while developing emotional intelligence.
#### Global Outlook
As an international school training students to excel globally, we foster an ethos of honesty, responsibility, tolerance and respect for others within the academic and Positive Education curriculum.
Sixth Form students take a leading role in celebrations such as International Day by sharing their own cultures and traditions with the rest of the community. As a multi-pathway school, we aim to make our students global citizens. 
#### Sixth Form Options Booklet
We have designed the below booklet to inform and support our students on the multiple pathways available to them. We are unique in offering A Levels, BTECs, and being an IB world school, we also offer the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme and the International Baccalaureate Career Related Programme. It is therefore, important to understand each one to make an informed decision.
